Rajasthan.name: Jaipur
Rajasthan.desc: Pink City — Amber Fort, Hawa Mahal, palaces, and vibrant bazaars.
Rajasthan.history: Founded in 1727 by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II, Jaipur is the world's first planned city with a grid layout.

Rajasthan.name: Jodhpur
Rajasthan.desc: Blue City — Mehrangarh Fort, Jaswant Thada, golden desert sunsets.
Rajasthan.history: In the heart of the Thar Desert, Jodhpur's iconic blue-painted houses and imposing fort define the landscape.

Kerala.name: Munnar
Kerala.desc: Rolling tea estates, misty Nilgiri hills, scenic Eravikulam National Park.
Kerala.history: Munnar sits at 1,600m elevation and is India's largest tea-growing region.

Kerala.name: Alleppey
Kerala.desc: Backwaters capital — houseboat stays on Kerala's famous canals.
Kerala.history: Alleppey has 1,500 km of interconnected waterways attracting tourists for houseboat cruises.
